materialDescription.nami: Made from Katsuragi cotton, Nami is a soft and easy-to-tie fabric, lighter in feel than Shushi or Yōhachi. Traditionally used for white and colored belts, it is also a popular choice for first-time black belts and kumite athletes who prefer a relatively easy-to-tighten obi that feels comfortable from the start.
materialDescription.shushi: Shushi surface is glossy. The core is thicker and sturdier than the regular Nami obi. At first, it is slippery and easy to unravel, but the edges tend to peel off easily, so it will settle in relatively quickly.
materialDescription.yohachi: Yōhachi fabric is made from high-quality cotton (grosgrain weave). The core is thicker and sturdier than the regular Nami obi. It is relatively hard to unravel from the start, and the edges are also hard to peel, so it is easy to wear and can be used for a long time.
materialDescription.silk: Silk is the finest fabric made literally from 100% natural silk. The surface is soft and glossy, giving it an elegant finish. Compared to Shushi, it is easier to tighten from the very beginning.

widthDescription.normal: This is a 4 cm wide belt. It is relatively easy to tie and becomes supple with use. It is often used by first-time black belts and kumite athletes.
widthDescription.special: This is a 4.5 cm wide belt with an extra layer of komi (core), giving it a thicker and more substantial feel. It is stable and makes the wearer look bigger. It is widely used by kata competitors and instructors, and is also commonly purchased as a gift.

modelDescription.tsubasa: An ultra-lightweight material that surpasses conventional standards. A special weaving method creates a textured inner surface, reducing friction and preventing the fabric from sticking to the skin. With the addition of elastane, the material provides approximately 5% stretch for greater ease of movement.
modelDescription.pinac-kumite: This best-selling karate uniform is at the core of all HIROTA products. It was a groundbreaking model that made a strong impression in an era when there was no clear distinction between kata and kumite uniforms. While it may not be specifically designed for kumite by today’s standards, especially as lighter uniforms continue to be developed, it remains lightweight, easy to move in, and versatile enough for a wide range of training situations. It is a favorite among many athletes and instructors.
modelDescription.mh-10: Made from thick No. 10 canvas woven with high-quality combed yarn, this karate uniform offers the weight and structure ideal for kata. It is also recommended for practitioners who find lightweight or medium-weight uniforms too light.
modelDescription.mh-11: Made from No. 11 canvas woven with high-quality combed yarn, this medium-weight karate uniform offers a balanced feel suitable for both kata and kumite.
modelDescription.mh-12: Made from a fabric approximately half the thickness of No. 10 canvas, this karate uniform offers an affordable option for beginners and fast-growing children.

About.title: hirota
About.intro: is a Japanese manufacturing house specializing in premium karate-gi and karate equipment.
About.history: Founded in 1965, the company has established a long-standing reputation for precision craftsmanship, material excellence, and uncompromising quality. HIROTA uniforms are trusted and worn by masters, instructors, and elite competitors at the highest levels of traditional and competitive karate worldwide.
About.tokyoTitle: 東京本社 Tokyo Head Office
About.tokyoAddress: NS Bldg. 1F, 5-33-6 Kamata, Ohta-ku, Tokyo 144-0052, Japan.
About.fukuokaAddress: 4-2-15 Sumiyon Bldg., Sumiyoshi, Hakata-ku, Fukuoka City 812-0018, Japan.
About.aichiAddress: 16-3 Rendai, Yonezu-cho, Nishio City, Aichi 445-0802, Japan.
About.hoursClosed: Sunday, National Holidays, and 2nd Saturday of each month: Closed
About.visitP1: To ensure proper attention and accurate measurement, please make a reservation before visiting any HIROTA location. Please call the branch you plan to visit and make a reservation in advance.
About.visitP2: If you would like to have your karate-gi measurements taken, please inform us of your height, weight, and preferred fabric type when booking. For measurement sessions, please wear or bring a T-shirt and tight-fitting bottoms (such as spats) to ensure accurate fitting.
About.visitP3: If you visit without a reservation or during busy periods with many appointments, we may not be able to assist you. Please make sure to book your visit in advance.
About.copyright: © {year} HIROTA CO., LTD. All Rights Reserved.
About.disclaimer: Self-initiated concept build. Not commissioned or endorsed by HIROTA.

modelDescription.tsubasa: An ultra-lightweight material that surpasses conventional standards. A special weaving method creates a textured inner surface, reducing friction and preventing the fabric from sticking to the skin. With the addition of elastane, the material provides approximately 5% stretch for greater ease of movement.
modelDescription.kuu: Even lighter than the Pinac Kumite, this ultra-lightweight fabric is thin, exceptionally soft, and compact, making it easy to carry.
modelDescription.pinac-kumite: This best-selling karate uniform is at the core of all HIROTA products. It was a groundbreaking model that made a strong impression in an era when there was no clear distinction between kata and kumite uniforms. While it may not be specifically designed for kumite by today’s standards, especially as lighter uniforms continue to be developed, it remains lightweight, easy to move in, and versatile enough for a wide range of training situations. It is a favorite among many athletes and instructors.
modelDescription.takumi: Made from the same threads used in Pinac Kumite, this fabric is woven at an extremely high density to achieve a firm, crisp finish. Thin yet structured, it offers excellent mobility and delivers exceptional snap. It is one of HIROTA’s most popular garments, favored by everyone from children to top athletes.
modelDescription.pinac-kata: Made with a thicker thread than Pinac Kumite, this medium-weight fabric is neither too light nor too heavy, making it a stable and versatile garment.
modelDescription.mh-10: Made from thick No. 10 canvas woven with high-quality combed yarn, this karate uniform offers the weight and structure ideal for kata. It is also recommended for practitioners who find lightweight or medium-weight uniforms too light.
modelDescription.mh-11: Made from No. 11 canvas woven with high-quality combed yarn, this medium-weight karate uniform offers a balanced feel suitable for both kata and kumite.

GiCustom.purchaseUnitNote: Jacket and pants are priced separately; buying both as a set is the best value.
GiCustom.bandNote: Choose the size band that contains your size — it sets the base price.
GiCustom.quoteExplainer: Sizes above 8 are made to a custom quote. Please contact HIROTA for pricing on this size.
GiCustom.measurementsNote: Enter the final garment measurements in cm. They are validated against the top of the selected band; F is recorded but does not affect sizing.
GiCustom.measurementsNoteQuote: This band is quote-on-request; measurements are recorded but not validated here.
GiCustom.collarNote: Available on kata models only. Not offered on kumite models.
GiCustom.hemsNote: One width & thickness applied to the whole garment. 4cm normal is the free default; kumite models allow normal thickness only.
GiCustom.highWaistNote: Enter the exact high-waist height in cm (up to 13cm). The price comes from the band it falls into.
GiCustom.highWaistTooBig: High waist must be 13cm or less.
GiCustom.highWaistNeedsValue: Enter a high-waist height (1–13cm) to continue.
GiCustom.elasticNote: Available on Tsubasa only. Can be combined with a high waist.
GiCustom.mfrLogoNote: Hirota logo can be embroidered on the jacket. No logo is added by default.
GiCustom.threadNeedsText: Enter text in at least one field, or clear the thread color.
GiCustom.shrinkageNote: Required — the garment is measured to order.
GiCustom.shrinkageRequired: Select a shrinkage option to continue.
GiCustom.bodyNote: Required sanity-check fields — stored on the order, not used to build the garment.
GiCustom.bodyNeedsAll: Enter height, weight and waist to continue.
